chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-5051 of 2023 Fayaz Khan Versus Abdul Ghaffar & others Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on CMA 24760/23 2. For orders on office objection No.19. 3. For orders on CMA 23108/23 4. For orders on CMA 23109/23 5. For hearing of main case. Dated: 08.11.2023 Mr. Zakir Hussain Khaskheli for petitioner. -.-.- Heard the counsel. In a suit bearing No.290 of 2016 filed before Senior Civil Judge Malir, a decree was passed on 20.09.2017; a challenge was thrown by moving several applications, such as Section 12 CPC, Order XXI Rule 26/29 CPC and Order I Rule 10 CPC, all of which were dismissed. Petitioner then preferred Revision Application No.22 of 2021 in relation to Section 12 and Order I Rule 10 CPC, which too was dismissed. Consequently this petition, which in any case is not a remedy, in view of above. Petitioner for the purposes of establishing fraud has attempted to disclose an agreement of sale dated 20.02.2005 with someone called Soomar to whom a sublease was executed by partners of Simra Corporation, but no performance sought since 20.02.2005, whereas plaintiff of the suit (respondent No.1 herein) sought performance of an agreement against M/s Rafi Mateen Builders. Performance granted vide decree, which was challenged, as referred above. No ground for fraud urged, which could satisfy the Courts below which performed/exercised jurisdiction vested in them. Intervention on the strength of mere a sale agreement, whose performance is also hanging in the ropes of limitation, cannot be permitted. Instant High Court Appeal as such is dismissed along with listed applications.

Intervention on the strength of mere a sale agreement, whose performance is also hanging in the ropes of limitation, cannot be permitted. Instant High Court Appeal as such is dismissed along with listed applications. Judge Judge
